lean_stdlib_notes.no_by_contra: by_contra is 'unknown tactic' in Lean 4.28.0 - use Nat.le_of_not_lt instead
lean_stdlib_notes.no_push_neg: push_neg not available without Mathlib - use Nat.not_le.mp or omega
lean_stdlib_notes.no_split_ifs: split_ifs not available without Mathlib - use by_cases h : cond
lean_stdlib_notes.omega_cant_min: omega cannot reason about Nat.min directly - use by_cases with Nat.min_eq_left/Nat.min_eq_right then rw
lean_stdlib_notes.nat_min_pattern: For jci proofs: by_cases h : ci_in <= ci_out; rw [Nat.min_eq_left h]; else have hlt := Nat.not_le.mp h; rw [Nat.min_eq_right (Nat.le_of_lt hlt)]
lean_stdlib_notes.nat_le_min_mpr: Nat.le_min.mpr <h1, h2> proves k <= min m n from h1 : k <= m and h2 : k <= n
lean_stdlib_notes.nat_not_le_mp: Nat.not_le.mp h where h : ¬(a ≤ b) gives b < a
lean_stdlib_notes.no_simp_min_eq_left: Do NOT use simp [jointCommittedIndex] then rw Nat.min_eq_left - use unfold then rw directly
lean_stdlib_notes.min_zero_simp: After rw [committedIndex_empty], goal becomes min 0 x = 0 or min x 0 = 0 - use simp to close
lean_stdlib_notes.barrel_file: formal-verification/lean/FVSquad.lean must import ALL module files for lake build to compile them
lean_stdlib_notes.import_placement: imports MUST be at the very top of the file, before any doc comments or code
lean_stdlib_notes.theorem_ordering: Define theorems BEFORE use - cannot forward-reference.
lean_stdlib_notes.list_isEmpty: List.isEmpty is a Bool function. Use 'cases outgoing with | nil => ... | cons h t => ...' to case-split on emptiness
lean_stdlib_notes.list_mem_cons_self: List.mem_cons_self takes NO explicit args in 4.28.0
lean_stdlib_notes.list_mem_cons_of_mem: List.mem_cons_of_mem takes the element as explicit arg: List.mem_cons_of_mem e he'
lean_stdlib_notes.not_mem_nil: List.not_mem_nil takes type arg not value - just use 'simp at he' to dispatch membership-in-nil goals
lean_stdlib_notes.cases_bool: To case-split on a Bool expression b: use 'cases hm : b with | false => ... | true => ...' to get hm : b = false or hm : b = true
lean_stdlib_notes.list_noconfusion: To prove x :: xs ≠ []: use 'by simp' or 'List.cons_ne_nil x xs'
lean_stdlib_notes.unfold_vs_simp: For if-then-else definitions, use 'unfold def' before 'cases' - simp may over-simplify
lean_stdlib_notes.open_namespace: Use 'open FVSquad.CommittedIndex' at the top of JointCommittedIndex.lean to access namespace defs without qualification
lean_stdlib_notes.unused_simp_args: Lean 4.28.0 linter warns on unused simp args - do not pass unused terms to simp only

critique_notes.main_semantic_gap: Voter lists use List Nat instead of deduplicated Finset/HashSet - theorems hold under implicit Nodup assumption not formally enforced
critique_notes.empty_outgoing_divergence: jointCommittedIndex empty-config returns 0 in Lean model vs u64::MAX in Rust - documented in JCI9/JCI10 and CORRESPONDENCE.md
